result:
Some mornings begin quietly, with only the low murmur of the heater and the timid songs of birds who misread the weather report. I like those starts because they feel like unclaimed territory, little moments nobody has decided to own. In that quiet I make coffee and watch the steam rise in slow choreography, pretending it is a kind of weather inside the cup. The thoughts that wander in are rarely grand; they are small questions about why mint is always green or whether anyone has ever truly enjoyed ironing shirts.

By midmorning the inbox unfurls like a parade, bright flags of urgency waving from every subject line. There is a rhythm to answering, deferring, and inventing polite delays. I wonder if musicians feel the same when confronted with sheet music, notes marching in black ink, requesting attention. Maybe the difference is that their work eventually produces sound, while mine produces a digital echo that disappears when servers are cleaned.

After work I wander to a park near the river. It is never dramatic: a few joggers, a dog carefully negotiating which tree to approve, teenagers sharing headphones. Yet something about the repetition comforts me. The river keeps moving without permission, ignoring deadlines, ignoring likes and shares. I try to imitate it, to let momentum be enough.

When evening arrives, the sun folds itself behind rooftops, and I return home with a pocket full of observations. They are not achievements, proof I was awake, paying attention, turning moments into testimony.

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want for 250 words.
